articles.title: May 2026 school holidays in India: State-wise closure list amid heatwave conditions - The Times of India
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Arunachal Pradesh
articles.full_text:
School Holiday May 2026: Schools across India are following a mix of gazetted holidays, weekend offs, and state-specific summer vacation schedules. Buddha Purnima, a gazetted holiday, falls on Friday, May 1, 2026, marking a nationwide closure of the month alongside regionally declared summer breaks, local observances, and varying school calendar arrangements across different states.
Rising temperatures and ongoing heatwave conditions in several parts of the country have already led to early summer vacations or revised school timings. In many states, academic calendars have been adjusted to reduce exposure during peak heat hours, with closures extending into June in multiple regions.
Gazetted holidays and weekly offs in May 2026Schools will remain closed on fixed national and recurring weekly holidays during May.
Date
| Occasion
| Coverage
|
May 1, 2026

|
May 23, 2026

| Nationwide in most schools
|
Summer vacations across heat-affected statesSeveral states have already announced or implemented summer vacations due to extreme heat conditions.
In Delhi-NCR, schools are scheduled for summer vacations from May 11 to June 30, 2026, with reopening on July 1. Authorities continue to monitor heatwave conditions, although no revision in dates has been issued.
Odisha has advanced its summer break from April 27 following directives issued in view of severe heat conditions.
Similar early closures are in place in Telangana and Andhra Pradesh, where schools began vacations from April 24, with reopening expected in mid-June.
Chhattisgarh initiated one of the earliest extended closures, with schools shut from April 20 to June 15, 2026. West Bengal schools in several districts also began vacations from April 22, with staggered closures continuing into May in some areas.

Revised school timings in heat-affected regionsStates such as Maharashtra, Jharkhand, Uttar Pradesh and Rajasthan have introduced morning-only school schedules in several districts to avoid peak afternoon heat. Timings typically range between early morning hours and noon, depending on local directives.
Kendriya Vidyalaya schools in multiple regions, including Delhi, Chandigarh and Lucknow, will observe summer vacations from May 8 to June 16, 2026, as part of their centralised academic calendar.
Across India, May 2026 school closures remain a combination of fixed national holidays, staggered state-wise summer vacations and heatwave-driven administrative adjustments, with schedules varying significantly by region.
